text:
Code of Conduct
This Code of Conduct sets out the standards of behavior expected of every employee, contractor, and
officer of Northbridge Analytics. Effective January 2026.

1. Respect and Non-Discrimination
Employees must treat colleagues, clients, and partners with respect regardless of race, gender, religion,
age, disability, or any other protected characteristic. Harassment or discrimination of any kind will not be
tolerated.

2. Conflicts of Interest
Employees must disclose any financial or personal interest that could reasonably be seen to conflict with
their duties, including outside employment, board positions, or significant investments in competitors or
clients.

3. Gifts and Hospitality
Employees may accept gifts from clients or vendors valued under 2,000 rupees. Gifts exceeding this value
must be declined or reported to the Compliance team and may need to be surrendered.

4. Confidentiality
Employees must not disclose confidential company or client information to any third party, whether during
or after their employment, except as required by law or with prior written consent.

5. Reporting Violations
Employees who witness a violation of this Code are expected to report it to their manager, HR, or through
the anonymous ethics hotline. Retaliation against anyone who reports a concern in good faith is strictly
prohibited.

6. Disciplinary Action
Violations of this Code may result in disciplinary action up to and including termination of employment,
depending on the severity and nature of the violation.

General Company Policy
This document consolidates overarching company-wide policies for Northbridge Analytics Pvt. Ltd.
Effective January 2026.

1. Company Overview
Northbridge Analytics is a data and analytics consultancy operating across three offices, with the majority
of staff working in project-based teams reporting to a delivery manager.

2. Holidays
The company observes 12 public holidays annually, published each December for the following calendar
year on the internal HR portal.

3. Performance Reviews
Formal performance reviews are conducted twice a year, in June and December. Reviews consider
delivery quality, client feedback, and collaboration with team members.

4. Workplace Flexibility
The company supports flexible working arrangements where they do not conflict with client commitments
or team collaboration needs. Specific rules for remote work are set out in the Remote Work Policy, and
leave entitlements are set out in the Leave Policy.

5. Equipment Provisioning
All employees are issued a company laptop, headset, and necessary software licenses upon joining.
Equipment must be returned to IT within five working days of the employee's last working day.

6. Grievance Redressal
Employees with a grievance related to compensation, working conditions, or interpersonal conflict may
raise it with HR, who will acknowledge receipt within 3 working days and aim to resolve it within 15 working
days.

Employee Handbook
Northbridge Analytics Pvt. Ltd. Version 3.2, effective January 2026.

1. Welcome
This handbook outlines the policies, expectations, and benefits that apply to every employee of
Northbridge Analytics. It is intended as a general reference and should be read alongside the specific
policy documents referenced throughout.

2. Working Hours
Standard working hours are 9:30 AM to 6:30 PM, Monday through Friday, with a one-hour lunch break.
Employees are expected to be available during core hours of 11:00 AM to 4:00 PM regardless of their
individual schedule.

3. Flexible and Remote Work
Employees may work remotely up to three days per week. Remote work does not require prior approval
from a manager, provided the employee remains reachable during core hours and attends all scheduled
meetings. Employees who wish to work remotely on a permanent basis should refer to the separate
Remote Work Policy.

4. Probation Period
New employees undergo a probation period of six months from their date of joining. During this period,
either party may terminate employment with two weeks written notice.

5. Code of Conduct
All employees are expected to act with integrity, treat colleagues with respect, and avoid conflicts of
interest. Full details are provided in the Code of Conduct document.

6. Leave Entitlement
Employees are entitled to casual leave, sick leave, and earned leave as described in the Leave Policy
document. Public holidays are declared annually by HR and published on the internal portal.

7. Expense Reimbursement
Business-related expenses such as travel, client meals, and approved software subscriptions are
reimbursable. Employees must submit itemized receipts within 30 days of the expense being incurred.

8. Information Security
Employees must follow the Information Security Policy at all times, particularly regarding password
hygiene, use of company devices, and handling of confidential client data.

Leave Policy
Applicable to all full-time employees of Northbridge Analytics. Effective January 2026.

1. Casual Leave
Employees receive 12 casual leaves annually, credited at the start of the calendar year. Casual leave may
be taken in units of half a day or more, with at least 24 hours notice to the reporting manager where
possible.

2. Sick Leave
Employees receive 10 sick leaves annually. Sick leave exceeding three consecutive days requires a
medical certificate to be submitted to HR within five working days of returning to work.

3. Earned Leave
Earned leave accrues at 1.5 days per completed month of service, up to a maximum of 18 days per year.
Earned leave must be applied for at least one week in advance except in emergencies.

4. Carry Forward
Unused casual leave cannot be carried forward beyond 5 days into the following calendar year; any
balance above this is forfeited. Earned leave may be carried forward up to a maximum accumulated
balance of 45 days, beyond which it is automatically encashed.

5. Leave During Notice Period
Employees serving a notice period may not avail earned leave without written approval from both their
reporting manager and HR.

6. Maternity and Paternity Leave
Maternity leave is provided for 26 weeks in accordance with applicable statutory requirements. Paternity
leave of 10 working days is available to be availed within three months of the child's birth.

Remote Work Policy
This document governs remote and hybrid work arrangements for all departments. Effective January 2026,
supersedes prior informal arrangements.

1. Eligibility
Remote work is available to employees who have completed their probation period and hold a role that
does not require physical presence, such as roles involving lab equipment, secure hardware, or in-person
client support.

2. Approval Process
Remote work requires prior written approval from the employee's reporting manager, submitted through
the HR portal at least three business days in advance. Standing or recurring remote work arrangements
must be reviewed and re-approved every quarter.

3. Frequency Limits
Employees approved for remote work may work from home up to two days per week. Requests for
additional remote days require approval from both the reporting manager and the department head, and
are granted only in exceptional circumstances.

4. Equipment and Connectivity
Employees working remotely must use company-issued laptops and connect via the corporate VPN at all
times when accessing internal systems. Personal devices may not be used to access client data under any
circumstances.

5. Availability
Remote employees must remain reachable via Slack and email during standard working hours and must
attend all meetings with video enabled unless otherwise agreed with their manager.

6. Revocation
Remote work privileges may be revoked at any time if performance concerns arise or if the employee fails
to comply with the Information Security Policy while working remotely.

Information Security Policy
Applies to all employees, contractors, and vendors with access to Northbridge Analytics systems. Effective
January 2026.

1. Password Requirements
All system passwords must be at least 12 characters long, include a mix of upper and lower case letters,
numbers, and symbols, and must be changed every 90 days. Password reuse across the last 5 passwords
is not permitted.

2. Device Security
Company-issued laptops must have disk encryption and endpoint protection software enabled at all times.
Employees must not disable or uninstall security agents without IT approval.

3. Data Classification
Data is classified as Public, Internal, Confidential, or Restricted. Client data is always classified as
Confidential or Restricted and may not be stored on personal devices, personal cloud storage, or
removable media.

4. Access Control
Access to production systems is granted on a least-privilege basis and reviewed quarterly by the security
team. Multi-factor authentication is mandatory for all accounts with access to Confidential or Restricted
data.

5. Incident Reporting
Any suspected security incident, including lost devices, phishing attempts, or unauthorized access, must
be reported to the security team within 24 hours via the incident reporting form on the internal portal.

6. Third-Party Software
Installation of third-party software on company devices requires approval from IT. Browser extensions that
request access to page content on internal tools are prohibited.
